words.meaningEn: Able to recover quickly from difficulties or tough situations.
words.example: She remained resilient despite facing many setbacks in her career.

words.meaningEn: Showing careful and persistent effort in work or duties.
words.example: The diligent student reviewed his notes every evening before bed.

words.meaningEn: Truthful and straightforward; not hiding one's thoughts.
words.example: He gave a candid opinion about the project without holding back.

words.meaningEn: Calm, peaceful, and untroubled.
words.example: The lake looked serene in the early morning light.

words.meaningEn: Feeling or showing appreciation for something received.
words.example: She was grateful for the help her neighbors offered during the flood.

words.meaningEn: Eager to know or learn something new.
words.example: The curious child asked endless questions about the stars.

words.meaningEn: Producing powerful feelings or strong, clear images in the mind.
words.example: He described the event in such vivid detail that everyone felt present.

words.meaningEn: Having a modest opinion of oneself; not arrogant or proud.
words.example: Despite his great success, he remained humble and approachable.

words.meaningEn: To continue doing something despite difficulty or opposition.
words.meaningBn: অটল থাকা, লেগে থাকা, চালিয়ে যাওয়া
words.example: He chose to persist with his studies even when it felt overwhelming.

words.meaningEn: Sending out light or warmth; visibly happy and glowing.
words.example: Her radiant smile made everyone in the room feel welcomed.

words.meaningEn: Free from pretense; genuinely meaning what one says.
words.example: His sincere apology helped repair their friendship.

words.meaningEn: Existing in large quantities; more than enough.
words.example: The market had an abundant supply of fresh vegetables this season.

words.meaningEn: The ability to understand and share the feelings of another person.
words.example: A good doctor shows empathy toward every patient they treat.

words.meaningEn: Acting with care and thought for the future; wise in practical matters.
words.example: It was prudent to save money before making such a large purchase.

words.meaningEn: Enthusiastic and keen to do or have something.
words.example: The team was eager to start the new project on Monday morning.

words.meaningEn: Willing to give more of something than is expected or necessary.
words.example: She was generous with her time, always helping those in need.

words.meaningEn: The quality of being clear and easy to understand.
words.example: The teacher explained the concept with great clarity.

words.meaningEn: The ability to do something that frightens you; bravery.
words.example: It took courage to speak the truth in front of such a large crowd.

words.meaningEn: Conscious and aware of the present moment and one's surroundings.
words.example: Being mindful of your words can prevent unnecessary misunderstandings.

words.meaningEn: The ability to wait calmly for something without becoming annoyed.
words.example: Learning a new language requires a great deal of patience.

words.meaningEn: Directing one's attention or effort toward a particular thing.
words.example: She stayed focused on her goal despite many distractions around her.

words.meaningEn: Able to adjust easily to new conditions or environments.
words.example: An adaptable worker can handle different tasks with ease.

words.meaningEn: The quality of being honest and having strong moral principles.
words.example: He acted with integrity, even when no one was watching.

words.meaningEn: A person who tends to be hopeful and confident about the future.
words.example: As an optimist, she always believed things would work out in the end.

words.meaningEn: Consistently good in quality or performance; able to be trusted.
words.example: A reliable friend is someone you can count on in difficult times.

words.meaningEn: To care for and encourage the growth or development of someone.
words.meaningBn: লালন করা, বিকশিত করা, পরিচর্যা করা
words.example: Good parents nurture their children's confidence from an early age.

words.meaningEn: To fill someone with the urge or ability to do something creative.
words.meaningBn: অনুপ্রাণিত করা, উৎসাহিত করা, উদ্বুদ্ধ করা
words.example: Her story of recovery inspired many people across the country.

words.meaningEn: Free from disturbance; calm and quiet.
words.example: They spent a tranquil afternoon by the riverside reading books.

words.meaningEn: Having the ability to make decisions quickly and effectively.
words.example: A decisive leader makes clear choices even under pressure.

words.meaningEn: To suffer something painful or difficult patiently over time.
words.meaningBn: সহ্য করা, টিকে থাকা, বরদাশত করা
words.example: They had to endure long hours of travel to reach their destination.

words.meaningEn: To grow or develop in a healthy or vigorous way.
words.meaningBn: সমৃদ্ধ হওয়া, বিকশিত হওয়া, উন্নতি লাভ করা
words.example: With proper care and support, young talent can truly flourish.

words.meaningEn: Holding firmly to something; not giving up easily.
words.example: The tenacious athlete trained every day despite her injury.

words.meaningEn: Fluent and persuasive in speaking or writing.
words.example: The eloquent speaker moved the entire audience with his words.

words.meaningEn: Well meaning and kindly toward others.
words.example: The benevolent donor funded a school in the rural village.

words.meaningEn: Having the ability to understand situations quickly and clearly.
words.example: The astute businessman spotted the opportunity before anyone else.

words.meaningEn: Deep sympathy and concern for the suffering of others.
words.example: The nurse treated every patient with warmth and compassion.

words.meaningEn: Able to express thoughts and ideas clearly and effectively.
words.example: She gave an articulate response that impressed the entire panel.

words.meaningEn: Able to adapt to many different functions or situations.
words.example: A versatile chef can cook dishes from many different cuisines.

words.meaningEn: To have a strong desire to achieve something great.
words.meaningBn: আকাঙ্ক্ষা করা, স্বপ্ন দেখা, লক্ষ্য রাখা
words.example: She aspired to become the first doctor in her family.

words.meaningEn: Logical and consistent; easy to understand.
words.example: His argument was coherent and well supported by evidence.

words.meaningEn: Great bravery in the face of danger, especially in battle.
words.example: The soldier was awarded a medal for his valor on the battlefield.

words.meaningEn: Having the ability to deal with sensitive situations carefully.
words.example: A tactful manager knows how to give feedback without hurting feelings.

words.meaningEn: Very deep in meaning, feeling, or knowledge.
words.example: The book had a profound impact on how she viewed the world.

words.meaningEn: Being careful with money and avoiding waste.
words.example: Her frugal lifestyle helped her save enough to buy a house.

words.meaningEn: Feeling or expressing great happiness and triumph.
words.example: The jubilant crowd cheered as the team won the championship.

words.meaningEn: Keeping careful watch for possible danger or difficulties.
words.example: The vigilant security guard noticed the suspicious activity immediately.

words.meaningEn: Firmly committed and unlikely to change in loyalty or purpose.
words.example: Her steadfast support helped him through the most difficult times.

words.meaningEn: Having a strong desire to learn or know about things.
words.example: The inquisitive student always asked questions beyond the textbook.

words.meaningEn: Courteous, kind, and pleasant in manner.
words.example: The gracious host made every guest feel comfortable and welcome.

words.meaningEn: Introducing new ideas or methods; creative and original.
words.example: The innovative engineer designed a solar-powered water purifier.

words.meaningEn: Admirably firm in purpose or belief; determined.
words.example: She was resolute in her decision to finish the marathon no matter what.

words.meaningEn: Having good judgment and understanding; wise.
words.example: The sagacious elder gave advice that saved the entire community.

words.meaningEn: Mental calmness and composure, especially in difficult situations.
words.example: She faced the crisis with remarkable equanimity and grace.

words.meaningEn: Courage and strength in bearing pain or adversity.
words.example: It took great fortitude to rebuild their lives after the disaster.

words.meaningEn: Full of light; bright and shining.
words.example: The luminous moon lit up the entire valley on that clear night.

words.meaningEn: The quality of being determined and not giving up easily.
words.example: His tenacity in the face of failure eventually led to great success.

words.meaningEn: Engaged in deep or serious thought.
words.example: She sat by the window with a pensive look on her face.

words.meaningEn: The quality of being open and honest in expression.
words.example: She appreciated his candor when he told her the truth directly.

words.meaningEn: Cheerful and optimistic; able to stay positive.
words.example: Despite the bad news, she remained buoyant and full of hope.

words.meaningEn: Showing great attention to detail; very careful and precise.
words.example: The meticulous editor reviewed every sentence before publication.

words.meaningEn: The highest point or peak of something; the best stage.
words.example: The artist reached the zenith of her career with that final painting.

words.meaningEn: Showing selfless concern for the well-being of others.
words.example: Her altruistic nature led her to volunteer every weekend at the shelter.

words.meaningEn: Having a feeling of longing or gentle sadness for something past.
words.example: He gave a wistful smile when he passed his old childhood home.

words.meaningEn: To give someone the authority or confidence to do something.
words.meaningBn: ক্ষমতায়িত করা, সাহস দেওয়া, শক্তি দেওয়া
words.example: Good education empowers people to make better choices in life.

words.meaningEn: Enduring pain or hardship without showing feelings.
words.example: He remained stoic throughout the ordeal, never once complaining.

words.meaningEn: Fluent and persuasive speech or writing.
words.example: The lawyer's eloquence convinced the jury beyond any doubt.

words.meaningEn: Truthful and frank; not afraid to speak one's mind.
words.example: The candid interview revealed the star's true feelings about fame.

words.meaningEn: Courage and determination in the face of difficulty.
words.example: It was pure grit that helped her finish the race with a broken shoe.

words.meaningEn: Showing moderation and self-restraint in behavior.
words.example: A temperate lifestyle helps maintain both physical and mental health.

words.meaningEn: Attractively lively and animated in character.
words.example: Her vivacious personality lit up every room she entered.

words.meaningEn: Having a clear and deep understanding of something.
words.example: Her insightful comments helped the team see the problem differently.

words.meaningEn: To regard with great respect and reverence.
words.meaningBn: সম্মান করা, শ্রদ্ধা করা, পূজা করা
words.example: People across the country venerate the founder of the nation.

words.meaningEn: Great energy and enthusiasm in pursuit of a cause.
words.example: She approached every task with a zeal that inspired her entire team.

words.meaningEn: Friendly, pleasant, and easy to talk to.
words.example: The affable teacher was loved by every student in the school.

words.meaningEn: To recognize or understand something clearly.
words.example: It takes wisdom to discern between what is urgent and what is important.

words.meaningEn: Generous and forgiving, especially toward rivals or enemies.
words.example: The magnanimous winner congratulated his opponent with a warm handshake.

words.meaningEn: To call upon something or someone for help or as an authority.
words.example: She invoked her right to remain silent during the questioning.

words.meaningEn: Wishing to do what is right; very careful and thorough.
words.example: A conscientious employee always double-checks their work before submitting.

words.meaningEn: Brave and heroic; showing polite attention especially to women.
words.example: The gallant firefighter rescued three children from the burning building.

words.meaningEn: To involve oneself deeply in a particular activity.
words.meaningBn: নিমজ্জিত করা, ডুবে যাওয়া, মগ্ন হওয়া
words.example: She immersed herself in the local culture during her travels abroad.

words.meaningEn: A calm and confident manner; graceful balance.
words.example: She delivered her speech with remarkable poise despite her nerves.

words.meaningEn: Severe or strict in manner; having no comforts or luxuries.
words.example: The monk lived an austere life with very few possessions.

words.meaningEn: To feel deep respect or admiration for someone.
words.meaningBn: শ্রদ্ধা করা, ভক্তি করা, সম্মান করা
words.example: Students across generations revere the great philosopher's teachings.

words.meaningEn: Having or showing strong and sincere feelings about something.
words.example: He was a fervent supporter of education for all children.

words.meaningEn: Very skilled or proficient at something.
words.example: She became adept at public speaking after years of practice.

words.meaningEn: To continue steadfastly despite difficulty or delay.
words.meaningBn: অধ্যবসায় করা, লেগে থাকা, হাল না ছাড়া
words.example: You must persevere through the hard times to reach your goals.

words.meaningEn: Loyal, reliable, and hardworking; strong and brave.
words.example: He was a stalwart member of the team for over fifteen years.

words.meaningEn: Having knowledge or awareness of something.
words.example: She was cognizant of the risks before making her final decision.

words.meaningEn: Full of energy, excitement, and cheerfulness.
words.example: The exuberant children ran through the park with endless joy.

words.meaningEn: Of imposing height; having noble or ambitious goals.
words.example: Despite his lofty ambitions, he remained grounded and humble.

words.meaningEn: Steady and careful effort put into work or duties.
words.example: Her diligence in studying every night paid off during the exams.

words.meaningEn: Having a friendly and pleasant manner.
words.example: The amiable receptionist made visitors feel welcome immediately.

words.meaningEn: To support or strengthen something.
words.meaningBn: শক্তিশালী করা, সহায়তা করা, উৎসাহিত করা
words.example: The coach bolstered the team's confidence before the big match.

words.meaningEn: Expressing or involving prolonged thoughtful consideration.
words.example: He sat in a contemplative silence before answering the question.

words.meaningEn: Skillful with hands or mind; quick and precise.
words.example: The dexterous surgeon completed the operation with perfect precision.

words.meaningEn: Cheerful and full of energy; overflowing with enthusiasm.
words.example: Her ebullient nature made her the life of every gathering.

words.meaningEn: Achieved too easily; superficial rather than thorough.
words.example: His facile answer showed he had not truly thought about the question.

words.meaningEn: Fond of the company of others; sociable.
words.example: The gregarious student quickly made friends on the first day of school.

words.meaningEn: Highly respected and considered sacred or holy.
words.example: The old library was considered hallowed ground by scholars.

words.meaningEn: In accordance with the highest standards; faultless.
words.example: Her impeccable attention to detail made her an excellent editor.

words.meaningEn: Having or showing good judgment; sensible and wise.
words.example: A judicious investor carefully studies the market before spending.

words.meaningEn: Relating to motion; lively and dynamic in quality.
words.example: The kinetic performance of the dancers left the audience breathless.

words.meaningEn: Expressed clearly and easy to understand.
words.example: The professor gave a lucid explanation of a very complex theory.

words.meaningEn: An independent-minded person who does not follow the crowd.
words.example: The maverick scientist pursued a theory that no one else believed in.

words.meaningEn: Quick and light in movement; agile and clever.
words.example: The nimble cat jumped from roof to roof with ease.

words.meaningEn: Not aware of or paying attention to what is happening.
words.example: He was oblivious to the noise around him while reading his book.

words.meaningEn: Producing a large amount of work, results, or offspring.
words.example: The prolific author published three novels in just two years.

words.meaningEn: To put an end to something, especially by using force or authority.
words.meaningBn: দমন করা, শান্ত করা, নিভিয়ে দেওয়া
words.example: The teacher managed to quell the argument before it got out of hand.

words.meaningEn: To put right something that is wrong or incorrect.
words.meaningBn: সংশোধন করা, ঠিক করা, ত্রুটি দূর করা
words.example: They worked quickly to rectify the error in the official report.

words.meaningEn: The state of being calm, peaceful, and untroubled.
words.example: The serenity of the mountain retreat helped him clear his mind.

words.meaningEn: Related to the sense of touch; perceptible by touch.
words.example: Children learn better with tactile experiences like handling real objects.

words.meaningEn: Steady and not changing; firm in purpose or resolve.
words.example: Her unwavering faith in her team helped them through the crisis.

words.meaningEn: To clear someone of blame or suspicion; to justify.
words.meaningBn: নির্দোষ প্রমাণ করা, সমর্থন করা, ন্যায্যতা প্রমাণ করা
words.example: New evidence vindicated him and he was finally set free.

words.meaningEn: Conducive to health or moral well-being; good for you.
words.example: She cooked wholesome meals for her family every single day.

words.meaningEn: To have an intense feeling of longing for something.
words.meaningBn: আকাঙ্ক্ষা করা, তীব্রভাবে চাওয়া, মন চাওয়া
words.example: She yearned to return to her hometown after years abroad.

words.meaningEn: Having great energy and enthusiasm for a cause.
words.example: The zealous volunteer worked twelve hours a day for the campaign.

words.meaningEn: The ability to make good judgments and quick decisions.
words.example: His business acumen helped the company double its profits in a year.

words.meaningEn: Gentle and kindly; not harmful or severe.
words.example: The doctor confirmed the growth was benign and nothing to worry about.

words.meaningEn: Able to be believed; convincing and trustworthy.
words.example: The journalist only published information from credible sources.

words.meaningEn: Showing fearlessness and determination; not discouraged.
words.example: The dauntless explorer ventured into territory no one had mapped before.

words.meaningEn: Famous and respected within a particular field.
words.example: The eminent scientist received a lifetime achievement award.

words.meaningEn: To understand something after much thought.
words.example: She could not fathom why he had left without saying goodbye.

words.meaningEn: Truly what something is said to be; authentic and real.
words.example: Her genuine interest in helping others made her a beloved teacher.

words.meaningEn: To control and make use of natural resources or abilities.
words.meaningBn: কাজে লাগানো, নিয়ন্ত্রণ করা, ব্যবহার করা
words.example: The engineers found a way to harness solar energy more efficiently.

words.meaningEn: To regard or represent something as perfect or better than it is.
words.example: It is easy to idealize the past and forget its many difficulties.

words.meaningEn: Fond of or characterized by joking; humorous.
words.example: His jocular manner put everyone at ease during the tense meeting.

words.meaningEn: Having a strong interest or enthusiasm; sharp and perceptive.
words.example: She had a keen eye for spotting talented young writers.

words.meaningEn: To lose vitality and become weak; to suffer from neglect.
words.meaningBn: দুর্বল হওয়া, নিস্তেজ হওয়া, অবহেলায় পড়ে থাকা
words.example: The project languished for years due to lack of funding.

words.meaningEn: An experienced person who advises and guides a less experienced one.
words.example: Her mentor helped her navigate the challenges of her first job.

words.meaningEn: Appearing casually calm and relaxed; not anxious.
words.example: She gave a nonchalant shrug as if the problem did not bother her.

words.meaningEn: Knowing everything; having complete or unlimited knowledge.
words.example: The novel uses an omniscient narrator who knows every character's thoughts.

words.meaningEn: Not easily upset or excited; calm and peaceful.
words.example: The placid lake reflected the mountains perfectly in the still morning.

words.meaningEn: Representing the most perfect example of something.
words.example: He is the quintessential entrepreneur — bold, creative, and relentless.

words.meaningEn: Strong and healthy; vigorous and sturdy.
words.example: The economy showed robust growth for the third consecutive year.

words.meaningEn: Optimistic in a difficult situation; positive and hopeful.
words.example: He remained sanguine about the future despite the recent setbacks.

words.meaningEn: Having skill in dealing with sensitive or difficult situations.
words.example: A tactful response can resolve even the most heated disagreements.

words.meaningEn: Present, appearing, or found everywhere.
words.example: Smartphones have become ubiquitous in modern daily life.

words.meaningEn: To confirm the accuracy or truth of something; to recognize.
words.meaningBn: বৈধতা দেওয়া, নিশ্চিত করা, স্বীকৃতি দেওয়া
words.example: The experiment validated the theory that had been debated for decades.

words.meaningEn: Playfully quaint or fanciful; given to unexpected ideas.
words.example: The whimsical illustrations made the children's book utterly charming.

words.meaningEn: Friendly to strangers or guests; hospitable.
words.example: The xenial family welcomed every traveler who knocked on their door.

words.meaningEn: Remaining young in spirit, appearance, or energy.
words.example: Despite his age, he had a youthful enthusiasm for learning.

words.meaningEn: Great energy or enthusiasm in pursuit of a cause or objective.
words.example: The young teacher approached every lesson with incredible zeal.

words.meaningEn: The practice of selfless concern for the well-being of others.
words.example: Her altruism led her to donate half of her salary every month.

words.meaningEn: Concise and exact use of words; shortness of time.
words.example: The report's brevity made it easy to read without losing any key points.

words.meaningEn: A person or thing that causes change or speeds up a process.
words.example: The new principal became the catalyst for real change in the school.

words.meaningEn: Done consciously and intentionally; careful and unhurried.
words.example: Every word in the speech was a deliberate choice by the writer.

words.meaningEn: To understand and share the feelings of another person.
words.example: A great leader can empathize with the struggles of their people.

words.meaningEn: The ability to predict and prepare for future events.
words.example: Her foresight in investing early helped secure her family's future.

words.meaningEn: The quality of being thankful; readiness to show appreciation.
words.example: Expressing gratitude daily can significantly improve mental well-being.

words.meaningEn: Boldness and courage; the ability to face hardship bravely.
words.example: It was sheer hardihood that got the climbers through the blizzard.

words.meaningEn: Extremely happy, peaceful, or picturesque.
words.example: They spent an idyllic summer afternoon in the countryside garden.

words.meaningEn: A special anniversary or celebration, especially of fifty years.
words.example: The school celebrated its golden jubilee with a grand ceremony.

words.meaningEn: A sharing of characteristics or origins; a close connection.
words.example: She felt a deep kinship with the other volunteers at the charity.

words.meaningEn: Sumptuously rich and elaborate; giving generously.
words.example: They hosted a lavish dinner to celebrate the company's anniversary.

words.meaningEn: Pleasantly smooth and soft; relaxed and good-natured.
words.example: His mellow voice made even difficult news sound reassuring.

words.meaningEn: Caring and encouraging the growth and development of others.
words.example: A nurturing environment is essential for a child's healthy development.

words.meaningEn: Ostentatiously rich and luxurious; wealthy.
words.example: The opulent palace drew thousands of tourists every year.

words.meaningEn: Continued effort to do something despite difficulty.
words.example: Her perseverance through failure eventually led her to great success.

words.meaningEn: To satisfy a desire or need; to put out a fire.
words.example: Nothing could quench her thirst for knowledge and discovery.

words.meaningEn: Light or heat as emitted from something; a glowing happiness.
words.example: There was a natural radiance about her that drew everyone toward her.

words.meaningEn: Having a strong moral sense; very careful and thorough.
words.example: The scrupulous accountant never cut corners on any financial report.

words.meaningEn: Tending to keep a firm hold; persistent and determined.
words.example: The tenacious researcher spent twenty years proving her hypothesis.

words.meaningEn: To raise the level of; to inspire and improve morally.
words.meaningBn: উন্নত করা, অনুপ্রাণিত করা, মনোবল বাড়ানো
words.example: Great music has the power to uplift the spirit in difficult times.

words.meaningEn: Great personal bravery shown especially in battle.
words.example: The commander was honored for his valor during the rescue operation.

words.meaningEn: To hold and use a weapon or tool; to have and use power.
words.meaningBn: ব্যবহার করা, প্রয়োগ করা, পরিচালনা করা
words.example: She wielded her authority fairly and with great responsibility.

words.meaningEn: A person who is attracted to foreign cultures and people.
words.example: As a xenophile, she had friends from over thirty different countries.

words.meaningEn: To produce or provide a result; to give way to pressure.
words.meaningBn: ফলদান করা, মেনে নেওয়া, উৎপন্ন করা
words.example: Hard work and patience will eventually yield remarkable results.

words.meaningEn: Great enthusiasm and energy for life or an activity.
words.example: She tackled every challenge with a zest that amazed her colleagues.

words.meaningEn: Enthusiastic and public praise for an achievement.
words.example: The debut novel received widespread acclaim from critics worldwide.

words.meaningEn: A source of light or inspiration that guides others.
words.example: Her courage became a beacon of hope for everyone in the community.

words.meaningEn: Pleasant because suited to one's taste or nature; friendly.
words.example: The congenial atmosphere of the office made everyone productive.

words.meaningEn: Able to withstand wear and tear; long-lasting.
words.example: The durable friendship they built in school lasted a lifetime.

words.meaningEn: To bring a feeling or memory into the mind.
words.meaningBn: জাগানো, স্মৃতি呼ব করা, অনুভূতি জাগ্রত করা
words.example: The old photograph evoked warm memories of her childhood summers.

words.meaningEn: Intense and passionate feeling for something.
words.example: She sang the national anthem with great fervor and pride.

words.meaningEn: To gather information gradually from various sources.
words.meaningBn: সংগ্রহ করা, ধীরে ধীরে জানা, তথ্য আহরণ করা
words.example: She was able to glean useful insights from hours of interviews.

words.meaningEn: A place of safety, refuge, or comfort.
words.example: The library was her haven where she could think and read in peace.

words.meaningEn: To catch fire or cause to catch fire; to arouse strong feelings.
words.example: The teacher's passion ignited a love of reading in every student.

words.meaningEn: Feeling, expressing, or causing great happiness.
words.example: The joyful reunion of the family after years apart brought everyone to tears.

words.meaningEn: To arouse or inspire a feeling or interest.
words.example: One inspiring teacher can kindle a lifelong passion for learning.

words.meaningEn: The state of being free within society from oppressive restrictions.
words.example: Every person deserves the liberty to express their own identity.

words.meaningEn: To be filled with wonder and astonishment.
words.example: She marveled at the intricate beauty of the hand-woven tapestry.

words.meaningEn: Having high moral qualities; admirable in character.
words.example: It was a noble gesture to give up his seat for the elderly passenger.

words.meaningEn: A set of circumstances that makes it possible to do something.
words.example: Every challenge is an opportunity to learn something new about yourself.

words.meaningEn: Having strong enthusiasm and dedication for something.
words.example: She was a passionate advocate for children's education rights.

words.meaningEn: Idealistic and unrealistic; pursuing impossible ideals.
words.example: His quixotic plan to end poverty overnight was admired but impractical.

words.meaningEn: The ability to recover quickly from setbacks or difficulties.
words.example: The resilience of the community after the flood was truly inspiring.

words.meaningEn: The quality of having good judgment and wisdom.
words.example: The elder's sagacity guided the village through many difficult decisions.

words.meaningEn: The quality or state of being calm and free from disturbance.
words.example: He found tranquility in long walks through the quiet forest paths.

words.meaningEn: Honest, morally correct, and adhering to strong principles.
words.example: He was known as an upright judge who never bent to pressure.

words.meaningEn: Having or showing high moral standards and good character.
words.example: A virtuous person does what is right even when it is not easy.

words.meaningEn: The quality of having experience and good judgment.
words.example: True wisdom comes from learning from both success and failure.

words.meaningEn: Friendly to strangers; welcoming and hospitable.
words.example: The xenodochial villagers offered food and shelter to every traveler.

words.meaningEn: A deep longing or desire for something.
words.example: There was a deep yearning in his heart to return to his homeland.

words.meaningEn: The time at which something is most powerful or successful.
words.example: At the zenith of her career, she was performing in front of millions.

words.meaningEn: Very enthusiastic or passionate about something.
words.example: She was an ardent supporter of environmental protection.

words.meaningEn: Large in quantity; giving generously and freely.
words.example: The harvest was bountiful thanks to the rains arriving on time.

words.meaningEn: To attract and hold the attention of someone completely.
words.meaningBn: মুগ্ধ করা, আকৃষ্ট করা, বিমোহিত করা
words.example: The storyteller captivated every child with her magical tales.

words.meaningEn: Devoted to a task or purpose; committed and hardworking.
words.example: The dedicated nurse worked double shifts during the health crisis.

words.meaningEn: Vivacious and enthusiastic; bubbly in personality.
words.example: Her effervescent personality made the whole office feel energized.

words.meaningEn: Growing and developing in a vigorous and healthy way.
words.example: The flourishing business expanded to three new cities within a year.

words.meaningEn: Having elegance and beauty in movement or form.
words.example: The graceful dancer moved as if she were floating on air.

words.meaningEn: Deeply and sincerely felt; coming from the heart.
words.example: She received a heartfelt letter of appreciation from her former student.

words.meaningEn: To light up or make something clearer and easier to understand.
words.example: The professor used vivid examples to illuminate the abstract theory.

words.meaningEn: Cheerful and friendly; full of good humor.
words.example: The jovial grandfather made every family gathering a joyful occasion.

words.meaningEn: Intelligent and well-informed about a subject.
words.example: The knowledgeable guide explained every detail of the ancient ruins.

words.meaningEn: Deserving praise and commendation.
words.example: Her laudable efforts to clean the river inspired the whole town.

words.meaningEn: The practice of being fully aware of the present moment.
words.example: Daily mindfulness practice helped reduce her stress significantly.

words.meaningEn: Feeling a sentimental longing for the past.
words.example: The old song made her nostalgic for her carefree childhood days.

words.meaningEn: Hopefulness and confidence about the future.
words.example: Her optimism in the darkest times was a source of strength for all.

words.meaningEn: Having or showing a clear intention or aim.
words.example: She made purposeful choices every day to move toward her dream.

words.meaningEn: The most perfect or typical example of something.
words.example: Her calm under pressure was the quintessence of true leadership.

words.meaningEn: Deep respect and admiration for someone or something.
words.example: The students listened in reverence as the elder shared her wisdom.

words.meaningEn: The quality of being regular, even, and not likely to change.
words.example: The steadiness of his voice reassured everyone in the room.

words.meaningEn: Complete in every detail; not leaving anything out.
words.example: A thorough review of the contract revealed several hidden clauses.

words.meaningEn: Remaining calm in difficult situations; not easily upset.
words.example: The unflappable pilot landed the plane safely despite the storm.

words.meaningEn: A person with original ideas about what the future will be like.
words.example: The visionary founder changed the way the world communicates.

words.meaningEn: Showing a quick and inventive verbal humor.
words.example: His witty remarks made even the most boring meeting enjoyable.

words.meaningEn: To hold in very high regard; to praise or glorify.
words.meaningBn: উচ্চে তোলা, মহিমান্বিত করা, প্রশংসা করা
words.example: The ceremony was held to exalt the contributions of the volunteers.

words.meaningEn: Lasting throughout an entire year.
words.example: The yearlong project finally came to a successful completion.

words.meaningEn: Great energy or enthusiasm for a cause or an objective.
words.example: The reformer pursued justice with unwavering zeal all his life.

words.meaningEn: An award or privilege granted as a special honor.
words.example: The scientist received the highest accolade in her field.

words.meaningEn: To produce flowers; to flourish and develop in a healthy way.
words.meaningBn: ফুল ফোটানো, বিকশিত হওয়া, উন্নতি করা
words.example: With the right support, every student can bloom into their best self.

words.meaningEn: To protect and care for something lovingly; to hold dear.
words.meaningBn: লালন করা, আদর করা, মূল্য দেওয়া
words.example: She cherished every moment spent with her aging grandmother.

words.meaningEn: To think pleasant thoughts as a form of escapism.
words.example: He would often daydream about sailing across the open ocean.

words.meaningEn: Sincere and intense in conviction; serious in intention.
words.example: His earnest desire to help others made him a beloved community leader.

words.meaningEn: A special or instinctive aptitude for doing something.
words.example: She had a natural flair for bringing out the best in other people.

words.meaningEn: To shine brightly; to appear as a faint sign of hope.
words.meaningBn: ঝলমল করা, আলোকিত হওয়া, চকচক করা
words.example: A gleam of hope appeared when the rescue team finally arrived.

words.meaningEn: Agreement and peaceful coexistence; a pleasing combination.
words.example: The two cultures lived in perfect harmony for centuries.

words.meaningEn: To shed light on a subject; to clarify and make understandable.
words.meaningBn: আলোকিত করা, স্পষ্ট করা, বোধগম্য করানো
words.example: The documentary helped illuminate the struggles of migrant workers.

words.meaningEn: An act of traveling from one place to another; a long process.
words.example: Life is a journey best traveled with curiosity and an open heart.

words.meaningEn: The quality of being friendly, generous, and considerate.
words.example: A small act of kindness can have a huge impact on someone's day.

words.meaningEn: Something handed down from the past; lasting impact.
words.example: The teacher's legacy lived on through the thousands she had inspired.

words.meaningEn: Having impressive beauty or dignity; grand and stately.
words.example: The majestic mountains stood tall against the clear blue sky.

words.meaningEn: To plan and direct a route; to deal skillfully with a situation.
words.meaningBn: পথ দেখানো, পরিচালনা করা, পথ খুঁজে নেওয়া
words.example: It takes wisdom and patience to navigate difficult relationships.

words.meaningEn: To succeed in dealing with a problem or difficulty.
words.meaningBn: জয় করা, কাটিয়ে ওঠা, মোকাবেলা করা
words.example: With courage and support, she was able to overcome her deepest fears.

words.meaningEn: Very great or intense; showing great knowledge or insight.
words.example: The loss had a profound effect on the entire family for years.

words.meaningEn: A long or difficult search for something important.
words.example: His quest for knowledge led him across five continents.

words.meaningEn: To emit light or energy; to clearly show a quality or feeling.
words.meaningBn: বিকিরণ করা, ছড়িয়ে দেওয়া, প্রকাশ করা
words.example: She seemed to radiate confidence and calm wherever she went.

words.meaningEn: To fly or rise high; to increase rapidly and dramatically.
words.meaningBn: উড়ে যাওয়া, উচ্চে ওঠা, দ্রুত বৃদ্ধি পাওয়া
words.example: After years of hard work, her career finally began to soar.

words.meaningEn: To grow vigorously; to prosper and do well.
words.meaningBn: সমৃদ্ধ হওয়া, ভালো করা, উন্নতি লাভ করা
words.example: Children thrive when they are loved, supported, and encouraged.

words.meaningEn: Without error; completely reliable and constant.
words.example: Her unfailing kindness made her the most loved person in the office.

words.meaningEn: The state of being strong, active, and full of energy.
words.example: Regular exercise restores vitality and improves overall well-being.

words.meaningEn: Friendliness and affection; a feeling of being welcomed.
words.example: The warmth in her voice made the frightened child feel safe.

words.meaningEn: Hospitable and welcoming to guests and strangers.
words.example: The xenial host made sure every guest felt at home.

words.meaningEn: A person who works diligently and with great loyalty.
words.example: He did yeoman's work keeping the organization running smoothly.

words.meaningEn: Enthusiastic and devoted effort toward a goal.
words.example: She pursued every project with the same infectious zeal.

words.meaningEn: To exist in large numbers or amounts.
words.meaningBn: প্রচুর থাকা, ভরপুর হওয়া, পরিপূর্ণ থাকা
words.example: Opportunities abound for those who are willing to work hard.

words.meaningEn: Extremely happy and peaceful; full of joy.
words.example: They spent a blissful week together by the sea without any worries.

words.meaningEn: The quality of someone's character or the level of their ability.
words.example: We need employees of the highest caliber to lead this project.

words.meaningEn: To impress someone greatly with brilliance or skill.
words.meaningBn: মুগ্ধ করা, চোখ ঝলসানো, বিস্মিত করা
words.example: The young pianist dazzled the audience with her flawless performance.

words.meaningEn: To give someone the courage or confidence to do something.
words.meaningBn: সাহস দেওয়া, সাহসী করা, উৎসাহিত করা
words.example: Her mentor's belief in her emboldened her to take the stage.

words.meaningEn: To create something strong through effort; to move forward.
words.meaningBn: তৈরি করা, গড়ে তোলা, এগিয়ে যাওয়া
words.example: They forged a strong friendship through years of shared challenges.

words.meaningEn: To emit a steady light; to look healthy and happy.
words.meaningBn: জ্বলজ্বল করা, উজ্জ্বল হওয়া, আলোকিত দেখানো
words.example: Her face seemed to glow with happiness on her graduation day.

words.meaningEn: Feeling or inspiring optimism about the future.
words.example: Despite the setbacks, they remained hopeful about the outcome.

words.meaningEn: To form a mental image or idea; to think creatively.
words.meaningBn: কল্পনা করা, মনে মনে ভাবা, স্বপ্ন দেখা
words.example: She could imagine a future where every child had access to education.

words.meaningEn: A feeling of great happiness and celebration.
words.example: There was jubilance in the streets when the team won the final.

words.meaningEn: Showing eagerness and enthusiasm; perceptive and sharp.
words.example: He had a keen interest in astronomy from a very young age.

words.meaningEn: To stay in a place longer than necessary; to be slow to leave.
words.meaningBn: দেরি করা, থেকে যাওয়া, আঁকড়ে থাকা
words.example: The scent of the flowers lingered long after they had wilted.

words.meaningEn: To provide someone with a reason or incentive to act.
words.meaningBn: অনুপ্রাণিত করা, উৎসাহিত করা, চালিত করা
words.example: A good coach knows how to motivate each player differently.

words.meaningEn: To encourage the development of someone or something.
words.meaningBn: লালন করা, বিকশিত করা, পরিচর্যা করা
words.example: The program was designed to nurture leadership in young women.

words.meaningEn: Continuing forward; making progress without stopping.
words.example: Despite the hardship, she pressed onward with unshakeable resolve.

words.meaningEn: A person who is the first to explore or develop a new area.
words.example: She was a pioneer in computer science at a time when few women entered the field.

words.meaningEn: To satisfy a need or desire fully.
words.example: Reading widely helped quench his endless curiosity about the world.

words.meaningEn: Glowing with happiness, health, or a strong positive energy.
words.example: The radiant bride walked down the aisle to a standing ovation.

words.meaningEn: The quality of being physically or mentally strong.
words.example: She drew strength from the support of her family and friends.

words.meaningEn: Not affected by the passage of time; enduring forever.
words.example: Her timeless advice on kindness still resonates with people today.

words.meaningEn: Not giving way to pressure; firm and determined.
words.example: Her unyielding commitment to justice earned her widespread respect.

words.meaningEn: Having great worth, usefulness, or importance.
words.example: The most valuable lessons in life are often the hardest ones.

words.meaningEn: Fully committed with sincerity and enthusiasm.
words.meaningBn: সম্পূর্ণ আন্তরিক, পূর্ণ নিষ্ঠাসম্পন্ন, হৃদয়ের সবটুকু দিয়ে
words.example: She gave her wholehearted support to every cause she believed in.

words.meaningEn: Relating to hospitality and warmth toward guests.
words.example: The xenial nature of the community made newcomers feel instantly at home.

words.meaningEn: A strong desire or longing for something.
words.example: The yearning for connection drives much of human creativity.

words.meaningEn: Great enthusiasm and energy for life.
words.example: He lived each day with a zest that inspired everyone around him.

words.meaningEn: To successfully reach a goal through effort and skill.
words.meaningBn: অর্জন করা, সাফল্য পাওয়া, লক্ষ্যে পৌঁছানো
words.example: With focus and effort, you can achieve anything you set your mind to.

words.meaningEn: A state of equilibrium; an even distribution of weight or attention.
words.example: Finding a healthy balance between work and rest is essential for well-being.

words.meaningEn: Feeling and showing sympathy and concern for others.
words.example: A compassionate leader listens before deciding and always acts with care.

words.meaningEn: Highly motivated and relentlessly focused on achieving goals.
words.example: A driven individual does not need external pressure to stay on track.

words.meaningEn: Lasting over a long period of time; continuing.
words.example: Their enduring friendship survived decades of distance and change.

words.meaningEn: Loyal, reliable, and consistent in affection or allegiance.
words.example: A faithful companion stays by your side through every season of life.

words.meaningEn: Innovative and pioneering; introducing new ideas or methods.
words.example: The groundbreaking research changed how doctors treat the disease.

words.meaningEn: Having the qualities of a hero; extraordinarily brave.
words.example: Her heroic effort to save the drowning child was celebrated by all.

words.meaningEn: Too powerful to be defeated or overcome.
words.example: Her determination made her feel invincible in the face of every challenge.

words.meaningEn: Having or showing good sense and sound judgment.
words.example: A judicious use of resources can stretch a small budget very far.

words.meaningEn: Having a kind and sympathetic nature.
words.example: The kindhearted woman always made sure no one went hungry in her neighborhood.

words.meaningEn: To set someone free from imprisonment or oppression.
words.meaningBn: মুক্ত করা, স্বাধীন করা, বন্ধন থেকে মুক্তি দেওয়া
words.example: Education has the power to liberate people from cycles of poverty.

words.meaningEn: Impressively beautiful, elaborate, or extravagant.
words.example: The magnificent sunset painted the sky in shades of gold and pink.

words.meaningEn: To provide the food or other substances needed to grow and stay healthy.
words.meaningBn: পুষ্টি দেওয়া, লালন করা, বিকশিত করা
words.example: Books and music nourish the mind in the same way food nourishes the body.

words.meaningEn: To shine more brightly than; to be better than others.
words.example: Her creativity allowed her to outshine every other candidate.

words.meaningEn: Having a composed and self-assured manner.
words.example: She appeared poised and confident despite the pressure of the interview.

words.meaningEn: Willing to consider or accept new suggestions and ideas.
words.example: A receptive student grows faster because they welcome every new idea.

words.meaningEn: Calm and peaceful; untroubled.
words.example: The serene countryside was the perfect place to rest and reflect.

words.meaningEn: The quality of holding firm to a purpose; persistent determination.
words.example: It was pure tenacity that kept her going through the hardest years.

words.meaningEn: Not subject to any conditions; absolute and complete.
words.example: A parent's unconditional love is one of the greatest gifts in life.

words.meaningEn: Full of energy and enthusiasm; bright and striking.
words.example: The vibrant market was filled with colors, sounds, and wonderful aromas.

words.meaningEn: Behaving in a friendly and warm way toward someone.
words.example: The welcoming atmosphere of the school put every new student at ease.

words.meaningEn: Very unusual or remarkable; beyond what is ordinary.
words.example: Her extraordinary talent for music was evident from a very young age.

words.meaningEn: The quality of being young or young-looking in spirit and energy.
words.example: She maintained her youthfulness through curiosity and constant learning.

words.meaningEn: Excessive enthusiasm or devotion to a cause.
words.example: Passion is admirable, but zealotry can blind one to reason.

words.meaningEn: To express an idea or feeling clearly and effectively.
words.example: She could articulate complex ideas in a way everyone could understand.

words.meaningEn: To mature and develop in a positive and healthy way.
words.meaningBn: ফুলে ফেঁপে ওঠা, বিকশিত হওয়া, উন্নতি করা
words.example: Under the right guidance, even the shyest child can blossom.

words.meaningEn: To acknowledge a special occasion with joy and festivity.
words.meaningBn: উদযাপন করা, আনন্দ করা, উৎসব পালন করা
words.example: Take time to celebrate even the small victories along the way.

words.meaningEn: Very loving and loyal; dedicated wholeheartedly.
words.example: He was a devoted father who never missed a single school event.

words.meaningEn: To make someone very happy and excited.
words.meaningBn: আনন্দিত করা, উৎফুল্ল করা, উদ্বেলিত করা
words.example: The news of her promotion elated the entire family.

words.meaningEn: A bold or elaborate movement or gesture; a period of thriving.
words.example: The arts scene in the city entered a new flourish after the festival.

words.meaningEn: Truly authentic; free from pretense or deceit.
words.example: Her genuine smile told everyone that she was truly happy for them.

words.meaningEn: To keep a feeling or thought in one's mind; to shelter.
words.meaningBn: মনে পোষা, আশ্রয় দেওয়া, লালন করা
words.example: She chose not to harbor any resentment and moved forward with peace.

words.meaningEn: To light up or clarify something that was once unclear.
words.example: A single mentor can illuminate the path for an entire generation.

words.meaningEn: Feeling or expressing great joy and triumph.
words.example: The jubilant students cheered when the results were announced.

words.meaningEn: Eager and enthusiastic; having a sharp mind.
words.example: She had a keen sense of justice and stood up for others always.

words.meaningEn: Continuing or enduring for a long time.
words.example: The trip created lasting memories that she carried for the rest of her life.

words.meaningEn: The force or speed of something in motion; growing strength.
words.example: Once the project gained momentum, it became unstoppable.

words.meaningEn: Deserving attention or notice; remarkable.
words.example: Her noteworthy contribution to the project was recognized by everyone.

words.meaningEn: To prevail over a difficulty or obstacle.
words.meaningBn: জয় করা, কাটিয়ে ওঠা, পরাস্ত করা
words.example: She overcame every barrier placed before her with grace and determination.

words.meaningEn: To continue steadily in an action despite difficulty.
words.meaningBn: অধ্যবসায় করা, লেগে থাকা, হাল না ছাড়া
words.example: The only way to reach greatness is to persevere through every obstacle.

words.meaningEn: A purposeful journey or search toward an important goal.
words.example: Her quest to become a doctor began the day she helped her injured neighbor.

words.meaningEn: A warm, bright glow; the quality of looking joyful and vibrant.
words.example: There was a natural radiance about him that made people trust him.

words.meaningEn: Resolutely firm and not changing in loyalty or purpose.
words.example: Her steadfast belief in her students never wavered, even in their darkest days.

words.meaningEn: A great victory or achievement; a feeling of great elation.
words.example: Finishing the marathon was a personal triumph she would never forget.

words.meaningEn: Being the only one of its kind; unlike anything else.
words.example: Every person is unique, and that difference is something to be celebrated.

words.meaningEn: The quality of being full of energy, brightness, and life.
words.example: The vibrance of the festival filled every street with color and music.

words.meaningEn: A feeling of amazement and admiration caused by something beautiful.
words.example: Children approach the world with a sense of wonder we should never lose.

words.meaningEn: To be exceptionally good at something; to surpass others.
words.meaningBn: শ্রেষ্ঠত্ব দেখানো, ছাড়িয়ে যাওয়া, অসাধারণ হওয়া
words.example: With the right mindset, anyone can excel in their chosen field.

words.meaningEn: To have a deep longing for something meaningful.
words.meaningBn: তীব্রভাবে চাওয়া, আকাঙ্ক্ষা করা, মন কেঁদে ওঠা
words.example: Deep down, every person yearns to be seen, heard, and understood.

words.meaningEn: The peak or highest point of achievement or success.
words.example: Reaching the zenith of your potential requires patience and relentless effort.

words.meaningEn: A strong hope or ambition to achieve something great.
words.example: Her aspiration to become a scientist drove every decision she made.

words.meaningEn: The quality of being well meaning and kind toward others.
words.example: His benevolence toward strangers made him a legend in his town.

words.meaningEn: Held very dear; treated with great love and care.
words.example: The old letters were among her most cherished possessions.

words.meaningEn: The quality of being committed to a task or purpose.
words.example: Years of dedication to her craft made her the best in her field.

words.meaningEn: To give greater knowledge or understanding to someone.
words.example: Travel has the power to enlighten and broaden one's perspective.

words.meaningEn: To grow or develop in a healthy and vigorous way.
words.meaningBn: সমৃদ্ধ হওয়া, বিকশিত হওয়া, উন্নতি করা
words.example: Given love and freedom, every human being can flourish beautifully.

words.meaningEn: Kind, courteous, and pleasant in manner.
words.example: She was gracious in both victory and defeat, earning everyone's respect.

words.meaningEn: Sincerely and deeply felt; coming from the heart.
words.example: His heartfelt thank-you moved everyone in the audience to tears.

words.meaningEn: To fill someone with the motivation to create or achieve.
words.meaningBn: অনুপ্রাণিত করা, উৎসাহিত করা, উদ্বুদ্ধ করা
words.example: A single act of courage can inspire an entire generation to be brave.

words.meaningEn: The process of moving from one stage of life to another.
words.example: Every great achievement begins with the courage to start the journey.

words.meaningEn: To arouse or inspire a strong feeling or interest.
words.example: The right book at the right time can kindle a passion that lasts a lifetime.

words.meaningEn: Something that brings clarity, hope, or understanding.
words.example: Even in the darkest times, there is always a light worth moving toward.

words.meaningEn: Having a serious, important, or purposeful quality.
words.example: A meaningful life is built not from success alone but from connection and purpose.

words.meaningEn: Having or showing fine personal qualities or high moral principles.
words.example: Living a noble life means doing right even when no one is watching.

words.meaningEn: Hopeful and confident about the future.
words.example: An optimistic mindset is one of the most powerful tools for success.

words.meaningEn: Having intense feelings and enthusiasm for something.
words.example: Be passionate about your work, and success will follow naturally.

words.meaningEn: To project a strong quality or feeling outward.
words.meaningBn: বিকিরণ করা, ছড়িয়ে দেওয়া, প্রকাশ করা
words.example: Choose to radiate kindness and watch how the world responds.

words.meaningEn: Calm, clear, and free of worry.
words.example: End each year with a serene heart, grateful for every lesson learned.
